prevalence.source: ECDC / WHO Europe (hepatitis E epidemiology summaries)
prevalence.trend: increasing recognition of zoonotic HEV (genotype 3) in several countries

incidence.value: reported cases increasing; true incidence higher due to under-testing

burden.dalyPerCapita: low overall; higher in immunosuppressed and chronic liver disease
burden.mortalityRate: Low overall; higher in acute-on-chronic liver disease

riskFactors: transfusion-related risk (rare; mitigated by screening in some countries)
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Anti-HEV IgM and/or HEV RNA; evaluation in acute hepatitis and unexplained transaminitis

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; testing often performed in hepatitis workups and in immunosuppressed patients
publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: substantial silent exposure; symptomatic cases are a minority
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Most cases self-limited; chronic HEV managed in specialist services

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Reference lab access for HEV RNA common; increasing routine testing in many hepatology services
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Supportive care; ribavirin for chronic HEV in immunosuppressed; food safety counseling (avoid undercooked pork)
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Under-recognition in primary care and variability in routine testing between countries
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Strong laboratory capacity and specialist hepatology networks to manage chronic cases
